member.name: Sir James Cleverly
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir James Cleverly MP
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir James Cleverly is the Conservative MP for Braintree , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government: Regulation

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is committed to meeting its obligation to evaluate regulations after they have been implemented, including to ensure they are achieving their objectives, remain necessary, and are having the intended effects. Where the Regulatory Policy Com...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government: Reward Gateway

writtenQuestions.answerText: The cost referenced relates to the February 2026 invoice from Reward Gateway (UK) Ltd, totalling £35,905, for Instant Reward Vouchers purchased in January issued under MHCLG’s Exceptional Performance Scheme for delegated grades. eCodes are purchased via R...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: West Yorkshire Combined Authority: Lexington Communications

writtenQuestions.answerText: The PRCA Register is run by the PRCA and is not a matter for government.

writtenQuestions.answerText: Guidance for local authorities on determining the eligibility of hereditaments for the Pubs and Live Music venue relief was published on 18 February 2026 and can be found at gov.uk here. It is for local authorities to determine whether individual properti...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coastal Erosion and Flood Control: Databases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to the publication entitled Changes to the Single Data List from April 2020, updated 8 May 2026, for what reason the data collection on Strategic Overview of Flood and C
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra and the Environment Agency (EA) discontinued this Single Data List collection because it has been superseded by new national flood and coastal erosion risk information, including the National Flood Risk Assessment and National Coastal Erosion Risk M...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Pride in Place Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the policy paper entitled Protecting What Matters: Towards a more confident, cohesive, and resilient United Kingdom, updated 28 April 2026, whether the £800 mil
writtenQuestions.answerText: The commitment of up to £800m over ten years to a further 40 areas where social cohesion is under pressure will be delivered as part of the Pride in Place Programme, bringing the total investment of the programme to up to £5.8bn.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: Mo Baines was appointed via fair and open competition and provided a full declaration of interests to MHCLG prior to her appointment as Lead Non-Executive Director. This was shared with the Department in line with the Code of Conduct for Board Members of ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Voting Behaviour
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what (a) discussions has his Department had with, and (b) representations has his Department received from, Labour Together / Think Labour in relation to its proposals for compul
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has had no discussions with, and received no representations from, Labour Together / Think Labour in relation to proposals for compulsory voting.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Proof of Identity

writtenQuestions.answerText: Applicants for electoral registration in Great Britain are required to provide a National Insurance number or, if they are not able to do so, the reason they cannot provide it. The applicant’s National Insurance number is checked to confirm that the detai...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: New Towns: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the support of the local authority is a necessary condition of a proposed development being part of the Government’s new towns programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local authority support is not a condition of a proposed development being part of the new towns programme.As per Annex C: Locations Methodology of the New Towns Draft Programme consultation, which can be found on gov.uk here, sub-criteria and guidance in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The hereditament level rateable value data and SCat code information is consistent between the published Valuation Office (VO) Rating List and the data held by my department. The data held by my department are provided by the VO to MHCLG ahead of publicat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the Core Spending Power table: final local government finance settlement 2026–27 to 2028–29, published on 9 February, and the associated council tax requirement
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government collects data twice a year on the business rates local authorities collect, once at the start of the year based on estimates, and then again at the end of the year based on what was actually collected in a given year. This is known as the N...

writtenQuestions.heading: 1 Carlton Gardens: Council Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the answer of 28 April 2026, to Question 124734, on 1 Carlton Gardens, whether the legal liability for the new council tax surcharge for the Deputy Prime Minist
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Rt Hon Member to the answer given to Question UIN101525. A public consultation on HVCTS is ongoing.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Elections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the gov.uk collection entitled Local government reorganisation: Policy and programme updates, updated 12 March 2026, whether he intends to hold shadow unitary e
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is continuing to take forward the programme of local government reorganisation and councils should continue to plan on the basis of the current timetable.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help tackle foreign financial interference in domestic politics from (a) Open Society Foundations and (b) think tanks and academics funded by foreign g
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government takes attempts by any country to intervene in democratic processes very seriously. It is, and always will be, an absolute priority to protect our democratic and electoral processes, including from covert political funding. The National S...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Petitions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his Department has considered reforming the law on election petitions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department currently has no plans to make reforms to the law on election petitions.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: In most cases, sufficient information will be publicly available on Companies House to determine the permissibility of companies and limited liability partnerships wishing to make political donations. The revenue test requires recipients to check that the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Aspire: Tower Hamlets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the Ministerial Envoys in the London Borough of Tower Hamlets have inspected the accounts and finances of Aspire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is not the Ministerial Envoys’ role to inspect the accounts and finances of political parties in the London Borough of Tower Hamlets. The Electoral Commission is the independent regulator responsible for overseeing elections and regulating political fi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The detail of day-to-day activities of ministerial appointees and their engagement with the local authority in intervention is not routinely reported to ministers, and I am not in a position to comment on their observations. The Envoys are continuing to w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

Name of donor: USC Schwarzenegger Institute for State and Global Policy Address of donor: USC Pol Price School of Public Policy, University of Southern, California, 35511 Trousdale Pkwy, Los Ángeles CA 90089 Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights LHR-LAX-LHR (£16,044.36), car rental (£806.91), hotel LA 3 nights (£988.32), car service from LAX (£343.83), USC apartment (£3743.36), Uber to LAX (£106.93), Flight LAX-SMF-LAX (£1364.08) and hotel in Sacramento (£461.10), value £23,858.89 Destination of visit: United States (Los Angeles and Sacremento) Dates of visit: 29 January 2026 to 22 February 2026 Purpose of visit: I visited Los Angeles and Sacramento accompanied by Lady Cleverly, as the guests of The Schwarzenegger Institute for State and Global Policy. I was invited as a fellow and guest lecturer and to meet with the British and American Business Council, as well as notable figures and politicians in the Housing, Building, Regulation and Political sphere. (Registered 5 March 2026)
Officer in the Reserve Forces. I have received no payments since my election. (Registered 4 June 2016; updated 27 October 2016)
Non-executive Board Member of the Parliamentary Knowledge Schemes. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 20 May 2025 (Registered 23 September 2025)
